title: trichera drymeja subsp. centrifrons
intro: Trichera drymeja subsp. centrifrons, also known as the 'Mistmaak' or 'Vaalblaar', is a plant species belonging to the family Menispermaceae. It is traditionally used in South African folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating rheumatism, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues. Trichera drymeja subsp. centrifrons can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments in traditional medicine.
